title: Norths Devils

The Devils, Norths, Norty Norfs, The Devs
Past Grammar (1891; 135 years ago (1891)) Northern Suburbs District (1933; 93 years ago (1933))
* [Albert Bishop Park](/w/index.php?title=Albert_Bishop_Park&action=edit&redlink=1 "Albert Bishop Park (page does not exist)"), [Nundah, Queensland](/wiki/Nundah,_Queensland "Nundah, Queensland")

[Queensland Cup](/wiki/Queensland_Cup "Queensland Cup")   [Brisbane Rugby League](/wiki/Brisbane_Rugby_League "Brisbane Rugby League")   [Hastings Deering Colts](/wiki/Hastings_Deering_Colts "Hastings Deering Colts")   [Mal Meninga Cup](/wiki/Mal_Meninga_Cup "Mal Meninga Cup")   [Cyril Connell Challenge](/wiki/Cyril_Connell_Challenge "Cyril Connell Challenge")

for short, are a [rugby league](/wiki/Rugby_league "Rugby league") club representing the northern suburbs of [Brisbane](/wiki/Brisbane "Brisbane"), [Australia](/wiki/Australia "Australia"). The team colours are sky blue, navy blue and gold. They play in the [Hostplus Cup](/wiki/Queensland_Cup "Queensland Cup"), and, through their predecessors, are one of the oldest clubs in Australia.

[Rugby Union](/wiki/Rugby_Union "Rugby Union") club, which was formed in 1891 as a separate Old Boys football club for [Brisbane Grammar School](/wiki/Brisbane_Grammar_School "Brisbane Grammar School"). It shouldn't be confused with the school team known as Past & Present Grammar (made up of students, teachers & past students) which competed from 1888 to 1890.[[1]](#cite_note-Brief_History_of_GPS-1) The club was quite successful in its early years, winning premierships in 1892, 1898 and 1899, as well as 1914.[[1]](#cite_note-Brief_History_of_GPS-1) Wallaby captain [Bob McCowan](/wiki/Bob_McCowan "Bob McCowan") was a Past Grammar player when he led the [national side](/wiki/Australian_national_rugby_union_team "Australian national rugby union team") in 1899.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Australian rugby league club, based in Brisbane, QLD

**Norths Devils**

The **Northern Suburbs Devils**, or **North Brisbane Devils**, or often simply referred to as **Norths** for short, are a [rugby league](/wiki/Rugby_league "Rugby league") club representing the northern suburbs of [Brisbane](/wiki/Brisbane "Brisbane"), [Australia](/wiki/Australia "Australia"). The team colours are sky blue, navy blue and gold. They play in the [Hostplus Cup](/wiki/Queensland_Cup "Queensland Cup"), and, through their predecessors, are one of the oldest clubs in Australia.
Norths have won 14 A Grade, 17 Reserve Grade and 14 Colts/Third Grade Premierships. They hold the record for most consecutive first grade [Brisbane Rugby League](/wiki/Brisbane_Rugby_League_premiership "Brisbane Rugby League premiership") [premierships](/wiki/Grand_final "Grand final"), winning six in a row between 1959 and 1964 and being crowned champions most recently in 2024.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Norths_Devils&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

### Before rugby league

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Norths_Devils&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Before rugby league")]

The first incarnation of Northern Suburbs was the **Past Grammars** [Rugby Union](/wiki/Rugby_Union "Rugby Union") club, which was formed in 1891 as a separate Old Boys football club for [Brisbane Grammar School](/wiki/Brisbane_Grammar_School "Brisbane Grammar School"). It shouldn't be confused with the school team known as Past & Present Grammar (made up of students, teachers & past students) which competed from 1888 to 1890.[[1]](#cite_note-Brief_History_of_GPS-1) The club was quite successful in its early years, winning premierships in 1892, 1898 and 1899, as well as 1914.[[1]](#cite_note-Brief_History_of_GPS-1) Wallaby captain [Bob McCowan](/wiki/Bob_McCowan "Bob McCowan") was a Past Grammar player when he led the [national side](/wiki/Australian_national_rugby_union_team "Australian national rugby union team") in 1899.

### Switch to rugby league

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Norths_Devils&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Switch to rugby league")]

Following the disbandment of club [rugby union](/wiki/Rugby_union "Rugby union") in Brisbane towards the end of the [First World War](/wiki/First_World_War "First World War"), Past Grammars, along with [Christian Brothers](/wiki/Past_Brothers "Past Brothers") and [University](/wiki/University_of_Queensland "University of Queensland"), joined the Rugby Football League in 1920[[2]](#cite_note-2)

### From Past Grammars RLFC to Norths

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Norths_Devils&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: From Past Grammars RLFC to Norths")]

Past Grammars, also known as Grammar Norths, won their first premiership in 1927, before becoming Northern Suburbs, following the introduction of District Football by the [Brisbane Rugby League](/wiki/Queensland_Rugby_League "Queensland Rugby League") in 1933.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

### Post-war

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Norths_Devils&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Post-war")]

In 1959 [Clive Churchill](/wiki/Clive_Churchill "Clive Churchill") captain-coached Brisbane Rugby League club Norths to a premiership, his training methods were carried on by Bob Bax who coached Northern Suburbs RLFC to become the first club in BRL history to win three consecutive first grade premierships when they defeated [Fortitude Valley](/wiki/Fortitude_Valley_Diehards "Fortitude Valley Diehards") 29–5 in the 1961 [grand final](/wiki/Grand_final "Grand final") in front of a then-record club crowd of 19,824 at [Lang Park](/wiki/Lang_Park "Lang Park").[[3]](#cite_note-3) Norths continued to dominate the decade with 7 grand final wins in 8 appearances. It would be another 11 years before they tasted success again.

Norths' [Queensland](/wiki/Queensland_rugby_league_team "Queensland rugby league team") representative player [Nick Geiger](/wiki/Nick_Geiger "Nick Geiger") was selected as the [Australia national team](/wiki/Australia_national_rugby_league_team "Australia national rugby league team")'s [hooker](/wiki/Hooker_(rugby_league) "Hooker (rugby league)") in the final of the [1977 Rugby League World Cup](/wiki/1977_Rugby_League_World_Cup "1977 Rugby League World Cup") tournament.

### 1990s

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Norths_Devils&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: 1990s")]

[![Logo in use during 1980s and 1990s](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/f/f9/Norths-Devils_LOGO.PNG)](/wiki/File:Norths-Devils_LOGO.PNG "Logo in use during 1980s and 1990s")

Logo in use during 1980s and 1990s

In the 1990s, along with many other Queensland clubs, Norths suffered heavily financially. Eventually it came to the stage that in 1998 they became a [feeder club](/wiki/Feeder_club "Feeder club") with [National Rugby League](/wiki/National_Rugby_League "National Rugby League") newcomers, the [Melbourne Storm](/wiki/Melbourne_Storm "Melbourne Storm"), which attracted much needed finance, as well as some quality players, as well as securing the Devils' future. Also in 1998, the club won its first First Grade premiership since 1980,[[4]](#cite_note-4) beating [Wests](/wiki/Wests_Panthers "Wests Panthers") in the Grand Final.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

### 2000s

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Norths_Devils&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: 2000s")]

In the [2006 NRL Grand Final](/wiki/2006_NRL_Grand_Final#Grand_Final "2006 NRL Grand Final") [Melbourne's](/wiki/Melbourne_Storm "Melbourne Storm") 17-man team had 13 players who had played with Norths in past years, including [Matt Geyer](/wiki/Matt_Geyer "Matt Geyer"), who won a Premiership with the Devils in 1998[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] and Melbourne in 1999. In the [2006](/wiki/2006_Rugby_League_Tri-Nations "2006 Rugby League Tri-Nations") [Tri-Nations](/wiki/Rugby_League_Tri-Nations "Rugby League Tri-Nations") series, 4 Norths players graduated to the Australian Kangaroos squad and 2 players, via Melbourne Storm, represented [New Zealand Kiwis](/wiki/New_Zealand_national_rugby_league_team "New Zealand national rugby league team").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*].

In 2008 the Devils signed a partnership agreement with the Brisbane Broncos which sees developing Broncos players train with and compete for the Devils in the Intrust Super Cup.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

In 2018 Rohan Smith was recruited to be the Head Coach of the club. In 2019 he led the Devils to the finals, with the team falling agonizingly short of progressing past the first round.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

In 2021, Norths won their first premiership since the 1998 season beating The [Wynnum Manly Seagulls](/wiki/Wynnum_Manly_Seagulls "Wynnum Manly Seagulls") in the Grand Final as well as achieving the minor premiership. Such success was replicated the following season when Norths won the [2022 Queensland Cup](/wiki/2022_Queensland_Cup "2022 Queensland Cup") with a 16-10 victory over club rivals [Redcliffe](/wiki/Redcliffe_Dolphins "Redcliffe Dolphins") marking The Devils first "back to back" premiership run in the Queensland Cup era.[[5]](#cite_note-5) Norths also won the 2024 Cup, also in a final over Redcliffe.[[6]](#cite_note-6)
Under Coach Dave Elliott on 6 October 2024, Norths claimed their first [NRL State Championship](/wiki/NRL_State_Championship "NRL State Championship") defeating the NSW Cup winners [Newtown](/wiki/Newtown_Jets "Newtown Jets") 20-18.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

## Home grounds

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Norths_Devils&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Home grounds")]

For many years Norths played out of Oxenham Park in [Nundah](/wiki/Nundah "Nundah"), but they moved to their current home ground, [Albert Bishop Park](/w/index.php?title=Albert_Bishop_Park&action=edit&redlink=1 "Albert Bishop Park (page does not exist)"), in 1969. Commonly referred to simply as 'Bishop Park', it borders the Schulz Canal in Nundah, and is named after club stalwart Albert Bishop, [MBE](/wiki/Member_of_the_Most_Excellent_Order_of_the_British_Empire "Member of the Most Excellent Order of the British Empire"). Norths has a licensed Leagues Club, Norths Leagues, at Bishop Park, as well as a second licensed Club at Anzac Avenue, [Kallangur](/wiki/Kallangur "Kallangur"), further north of Brisbane.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

## Season summaries

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Norths_Devils&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Season summaries")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f8/Easts_vs_Norths_QLD_league_May_2014_02.jpg/250px-Easts_vs_Norths_QLD_league_May_2014_02.jpg)](/wiki/File:Easts_vs_Norths_QLD_league_May_2014_02.jpg)

[Easts Tigers](/wiki/Eastern_Suburbs_Tigers "Eastern Suburbs Tigers") versus Norths Devils at [Langlands Park](/wiki/Langlands_Park "Langlands Park"), Brisbane Australia. May 4, 2014.

## Greatest Team

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Norths_Devils&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Greatest Team")]

On 16 August 2008 in the year of the Centenary of Rugby League, Norths Devils announced their greatest team ever. The team is made up of players in their entire 75-year tenure.[[9]](#cite_note-9)

1. [Harry Bates](/w/index.php?title=Harry_Bates_(rugby_league)&action=edit&redlink=1 "Harry Bates (rugby league) (page does not exist)")
2. [Fonda Metassa](/wiki/Fonda_Metassa "Fonda Metassa")
3. [Jack Reardon](/wiki/Jack_Reardon_(rugby_league) "Jack Reardon (rugby league)")
4. [Henry Hegarty](/w/index.php?title=Henry_Hegarty&action=edit&redlink=1 "Henry Hegarty (page does not exist)")
5. [Joe Kilroy](/wiki/Joe_Kilroy "Joe Kilroy")
6. [Bill Pearson](/wiki/Bill_Pearson_(rugby_league) "Bill Pearson (rugby league)")
7. [Jack Stapleton](/w/index.php?title=Jack_Stapleton_(rugby_league)&action=edit&redlink=1 "Jack Stapleton (rugby league) (page does not exist)")
8. [Vic Rudd](/wiki/Vic_Rudd "Vic Rudd")
9. [Cameron Smith](/wiki/Cameron_Smith_(rugby_league,_born_1983) "Cameron Smith (rugby league, born 1983)")
10. [Lloyd Weier](/wiki/Lloyd_Weier "Lloyd Weier")
11. [Trevor Gillmeister](/wiki/Trevor_Gillmeister "Trevor Gillmeister")
12. [Mark Graham](/wiki/Mark_Graham_(rugby_league) "Mark Graham (rugby league)")
13. [Edward "Babe" Collins](/wiki/Edward_Collins_(rugby_league) "Edward Collins (rugby league)")
14. [Mark Murray](/wiki/Mark_Murray_(rugby_league) "Mark Murray (rugby league)")
15. [Greg Inglis](/wiki/Greg_Inglis "Greg Inglis")
16. [Greg Conescu](/wiki/Greg_Conescu "Greg Conescu")
17. [Ian Massie](/w/index.php?title=Ian_Massie&action=edit&redlink=1 "Ian Massie (page does not exist)")

Coach:

1. [Bob Bax](/wiki/Bob_Bax "Bob Bax")

Manager:

1. [Albert Bishop](/wiki/Albert_Bishop "Albert Bishop")
